description: The receiving-inspection / verification record (ISO 8.6) that Quality raises against a Goods Receipt on the materials path: it reviews the supplier documents (COA, MSDS), tests a physical sample at the risk-based inspection level, and records the approve/reject QC decision that gates whether the lot becomes a GRN or diverts to an NCR. Lives between the store handoff and the GRN. The COA-received check is a hard block on the materials path: with no COA on file inspection cannot proceed and no GRN may be raised. The spares/services path has no inspection (Engineering receives with no QC and no GRN).

fields.name: identifier
fields.label: Inspection No.
fields.validation: system pattern; unique; immutable once raised
fields.purpose: Stable key for the inspection event so the QC decision, conformity evidence and any NCR it raises all trace to one verification record and back to the GRN, requisition and supplier; the handle an auditor reads for release verification (ISO 8.6).

fields.name: grnId
fields.label: Goods Receipt
fields.validation: resolves to a GRN on the materials path; not null
fields.purpose: Anchors the inspection to the receipt it verifies so the approve gate releases that specific lot to GRN; one GRN carries one inspection on the materials path and only an approved inspection lets the GRN be raised (axiom A11 entry).

fields.name: requisitionReference
fields.label: Requisition
fields.validation: resolves to the originating requisition; inherited from the GRN; read-only
fields.purpose: QC and QA always inspect against the requisition, not the PO (the PO stays procurement-only); this keeps the verification traceable to the originating demand spine (axiom A1).

fields.name: supplierId
fields.label: Supplier
fields.purpose: Names the supplier whose lot is being verified so a reject feeds the quality dimension of the supplier scorecard and the four-factor perfect-order metric, and seeds the NCR / CAPA / re-evaluation loop (axiom A11).

fields.name: coaReceived
fields.label: COA Received
fields.validation: must be true before inspection or GRN may proceed on the materials path (HARD BLOCK); mandatory true for regulated / OTC / pharma items
fields.purpose: The hard gate: no Certificate of Analysis on file means no inspection and no GRN; the buyer chases the supplier in a loop until it is on file (ISO 8.4.3(b)/8.6). For regulated items this is a mandatory release condition, not a soft check.

fields.name: msdsReceived
fields.label: MSDS Received
fields.mandatoryCondition: set true when a Material Safety Data Sheet is required for the item (chemicals / hazardous / DG)
fields.validation: boolean; required true for hazardous / DG items before inspection proceeds
fields.purpose: Confirms the safety data sheet is on file for chemical / hazardous material so handling, storage and DG declaration are compliant; a document-completeness check QC reviews alongside the COA at receiving inspection.

fields.name: sampleSent
fields.label: Sample Sent to QC
fields.validation: true when the receiving store has sent a physical sample to Quality for testing
fields.purpose: Records the store-to-QC sample handoff that starts the test; the materials path is store receives, sends COA plus sample to QC, QC inspects, so this marks the inspection can begin.

fields.name: inspectionLevel
fields.label: Inspection Level
fields.mandatoryCondition: set per item criticality on the materials path
fields.validation: one of the risk-based levels per ISO 8.4.2 item criticality
fields.purpose: Sets how intensively the lot is verified (skip-lot / sampling / 100% / source inspection / certificate-of-conformance only) by item criticality, so verification effort matches risk (ISO 8.4.2); a low-risk item may clear on CoC while a critical one needs 100%.

fields.name: qcDecision
fields.label: QC Decision
fields.validation: approve required before the GRN may be raised on the materials path; reject raises an NCR (quarantine) instead of a GRN
fields.purpose: The QC release gate: only an approved lot becomes a GRN; a rejected lot diverts to NCR / quarantine and never produces a receipt; the single decision that opens the ISO 8.6 to 8.7 branch (axiom A11).

fields.name: defectQuantity
fields.label: Defect Quantity
fields.purpose: The quantity found non-conforming at test; sizes the reject and seeds the percentNonConformance and the affected quantity carried onto the NCR and any return.

fields.name: inspectionNotes
fields.label: Inspection Notes
fields.purpose: Free-text findings (label or moisture issues, off-spec observations) retained as conformity evidence; the human-readable record QC keeps for the release decision and that seeds the NCR description if rejected.

fields.name: testAttachments
fields.label: Test Reports
fields.validation: document / image files; test reports and lab results
fields.purpose: The retained conformity evidence (lab reports, test results, photos) that ISO 8.6 requires QC to keep with traceability to the authorizing person; the proof behind the approve/reject decision.

fields.name: inspectionDate
fields.label: Inspection Date
fields.validation: valid date; tenant timezone; display DD-MMM-YYYY; may be 10-15 days after receipt for late-surfacing issues
fields.purpose: The date the lot was inspected; orders the QC event and shows the inspection lag (most lots clear same/next day, some label or moisture issues surface 10-15 days later) for the quality cycle-time view.

fields.name: inspectorId
fields.label: Inspector
fields.validation: from authenticated profile; a Quality-role user; read-only
fields.purpose: Records the authorizing person who released or rejected the lot, the traceability-to-person ISO 8.6 requires on the verification record and on any NCR raised from it.

invariants: A1 inspection traces to the immutable requisition identifier
A11 QC gate / no-COA hard block precedes GRN; reject raises NCR (CAPA loop entry)
A13 audit + SSE on every inspection decision
A14 a reject feeds the quality dimension of the scorecard and four-factor perfect-order
A15 COA / document completeness is a hard release gate, not a weighted input
inspection is on the materials path only; spares/services have no inspection and no GRN
